questions.question: What are the major physiographic divisions of India?
questions.correct_answer: The Himalayas, Northern Plains, Peninsular Plateau, Indian Desert, Coastal Plains, and Islands
questions.explanation: India’s landforms are divided into six major physiographic divisions based on geological structure and relief features.

questions.question: What type of mountains are the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas were formed due to tectonic plate movements and are geologically young fold mountains.

questions.question: Name the three parallel ranges of the Himalayas.
questions.explanation: The Himalayan system consists of three main parallel ranges differing in height and structure.

questions.question: Which river systems form the Northern Plains?
questions.correct_answer: The Indus, Ganga, and Brahmaputra river systems
questions.explanation: These rivers deposit alluvial soil over millions of years forming the fertile Northern Plains.

questions.question: What is the Bhabar region?
questions.correct_answer: A narrow belt of pebbles deposited by rivers along the foothills of the Shiwaliks
questions.explanation: In this region, rivers disappear into the ground due to porous deposits.

questions.question: What is the Terai region?
questions.correct_answer: A wet, marshy and swampy region south of the Bhabar belt
questions.explanation: Rivers re-emerge in this region creating dense forests and fertile land.

questions.question: What is the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.correct_answer: An ancient tableland composed of igneous and metamorphic rocks
questions.explanation: It is one of the oldest landmasses of the earth formed from the Gondwana land.

questions.question: What are the two divisions of the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.explanation: The plateau is divided into these two main regions based on location and relief features.

questions.question: Which ghats are higher: Western Ghats or Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats are higher and more continuous compared to the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: Which is the main river flowing in the Indian Desert?
questions.explanation: The Luni is the only large river flowing through the Indian Desert region.

notes.title: The Himalayas
notes.content: The Himalayas are the highest mountain range in the world and act as a natural barrier protecting India from cold winds. They are also the source of many major rivers.

notes.title: Northern Plains
notes.content: The Northern Plains are fertile alluvial plains formed by river deposits and support intensive agriculture and dense population.

notes.title: Peninsular Plateau
notes.content: The Peninsular Plateau is rich in mineral resources and forms the backbone of India’s industrial development.

notes.title: Coastal Plains
notes.content: The coastal plains lie along the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal and are important for ports, fishing, and agriculture.

notes.title: Island Groups
notes.content: India has two island groups: Lakshadweep in the Arabian Sea and Andaman & Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal.

facts.fact: The Himalayas extend for about 2400 km across northern India.
The Northern Plains cover an area of about 7 lakh square kilometers.
Anai Mudi is the highest peak in the Western Ghats.
The Lakshadweep Islands are formed mainly by coral deposits.
